---
title: Build A Browser App
description: Start with camera rPPG; add browser EEG if you need brain signals; use Web Bluetooth to connect a Muse-compatible headset.
---

If you already finished [Build Your First Elata App](/tutorials/first-app), the
intended next step is **camera (rPPG)**—see that page’s *Where To Go Next* for
scaffold vs existing-app links. This page collects the same **scaffold or
integrate** choices in one view, in the **recommended priority order**.

**Primary:** most apps should start with **camera-based rPPG** (no extra
hardware). **Secondary:** add **browser EEG** when your product needs
brain-signal analysis. **Bluetooth:** add **`eeg-web-ble`** when you need **live
headset streaming**—it is the transport that connects a Muse-compatible device
to the EEG stack (always alongside `eeg-web`).

## How This Site Splits Docs

| Doc type | What it is | When to use it |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Scaffold** | Commands from [Quickstart](/quickstart) using `create-elata-demo` templates | New app, evaluation, or you want a reference repo before touching your product |
| **Step-by-step tutorial** | Numbered steps (install → minimal code → WASM/assets checks) for **existing** apps | You already have a bundler app and want a verified integration path |
| **Integration overview** | Shorter conceptual page: typical flow, minimal snippet, links | You read once for mental model and package boundaries, then use reference pages |

Tutorials and overviews cover similar ground at different depths—use **one primary path** (scaffold *or* tutorial *or* overview), not all three in sequence unless you are comparing behavior.

## Pick Your Workflow

### 1. Primary: Camera-based rPPG

| Path | Where to go |
| --- | --- |
| Scaffold | [Quickstart](/quickstart) → template `rppg-web-demo` |
| Step-by-step (existing app) | [Add Camera-Based rPPG To An Existing Browser App](/tutorials/rppg-existing-app) |
| Integration overview | [rPPG In A Browser App](/guides/rppg-browser) |

### 2. Optional: Browser EEG (WASM APIs, synthetic or your own samples)

| Path | Where to go |
| --- | --- |
| Scaffold | [Quickstart](/quickstart) → template `eeg-web-demo` |
| Step-by-step (existing app) | [Add EEG To An Existing Browser App](/tutorials/eeg-existing-app) |
| Integration overview | [EEG In A Browser App](/guides/eeg-browser) |

### 3. Enable Bluetooth headset: Web Bluetooth + EEG

Use this when you already want live data from a **Muse-compatible** headset in
Chrome or Edge. It is not a separate “app type” from EEG: **`eeg-web-ble` turns
on Bluetooth transport** on top of **`eeg-web`**.

| Path | Where to go |
| --- | --- |
| Scaffold | [Quickstart](/quickstart) → template `eeg-web-ble-demo` |
| Step-by-step (existing app) | [Stream Muse-Compatible EEG Over Web Bluetooth](/tutorials/eeg-ble-live-stream) |
| Integration overview | [Web Bluetooth With Supported Devices](/guides/web-bluetooth) |

If you have not wired the EEG WASM path yet, do **section 2** (or its
tutorial) before the BLE live-stream walkthrough.

## Onboarding Tutorial (All Templates)

[Build Your First Elata App](/tutorials/first-app) scaffolds one template end
to end. When you are done, it sends you **first to camera (rPPG)**, then
**optional EEG**, then **Bluetooth headset transport** for EEG, each with the
same **new app vs existing app** split.

## Package Names (Reminder)

See [Package Selection](/package-selection) for when to depend on each published package and paths to avoid.
